overview.description: Algorithmic trading software company using advanced mathematics and ML for market making
overview.description_long: Qminers develops proprietary algorithmic trading software that trades autonomously on global financial markets based on complex mathematical models, statistical methods and optimisation algorithms. The company focuses on market making — narrowing bid-ask spreads to improve market efficiency. They use C++, Python, deep learning, and FPGA technology. Their software runs under a partnership with a major US trading firm, with Qminers receiving a significant profit share. Founded in 2012, the company has never had a loss-making month.
overview.tagline: We develop software for algorithmic trading

history.origin_story: Founded in 2012 in Kutná Hora by Petr Zahradník (econometrics, Charles University) and Miloš Krejník. Instead of being hired by a US trading firm's headhunter, they proposed an independent software licensing deal and kept autonomy.

key_milestones.event: Launched trading software written in C++
First couple of million dollars operating through US partner
Became independent and licensed out software; moved to Prague
Learned to work with FPGA cards; donated CZK 2M for hospital ventilators
Contributed CZK 5M towards military equipment for Ukraine
Record year for total profits and charitable contributions
Best results in company history; established Qminers Foundation; renovated Špork Palace offices

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Qminers' core value is proprietary real-time market making algorithms built on deep quantitative finance expertise, C++ microsecond execution, and FPGA hardware — none of which can be replicated by general-purpose LLMs. Their moat lies in domain knowledge, proprietary data pipelines, and ultra-low latency engineering.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Market making is a well-established, profitable segment of global financial markets. Qminers has an active partnership with a major US trading firm and has operated profitably without a single loss-making month since 2012, demonstrating robust and durable demand.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Qminers is fully self-funded and bootstrapped, profitable from inception with no external investors. The company explicitly states it has never had a loss-making month in over a decade of operation.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Qminers is a profitable, bootstrapped company with a deep technical moat in quantitative finance, zero funding risk, and a business model insulated from commoditisation by LLMs. The main risk would be a structural change in market microstructure, but their diversified global market exposure and ongoing ML/deep learning investment mitigate this.

metadata.notes: Bootstrapped proprietary trading firm with unique licensing business model — not a typical SaaS. Team size ~40 per company website, LinkedIn shows 41. No external funding or public revenue data; company describes consistent profitability since 2012.
